title: 4 Tracing Interac e芒聙聭Transfer Transactions via OAS
body_text:
4	Tracing
Interac
e芒聙聭Transfer Transactions via OAS
4.1	Overview of Tracing
Interac
e芒聙聭Transfer Transactions via OAS
Use OAS to search for the following types of
Interac
e-Transfer
脗庐
transactions that were created within the last two years and meet the following criteria:
the
Interac
e-Transfer transaction* was created by a customer via your financial institution芒聙聶s online banking platform; or
the
Interac
e-Transfer receive transaction was deposited to a customer芒聙聶s banking account with you, either through an interactive transfer, where the customer correctly answered the security question, or by direct deposit through Autodeposit or Account Number Routing.
*This includes
Interac
e-Transfer Request Money transactions.
Note:	Use the OAS search function together with your
Interac
e-Transfer reconciliation files to obtain reference numbers that can be used for searching transactions in OAS by transaction references (for example, transfers received that can芒聙聶t be matched to a customer
Interac
profile registered to your financial institution). For information on using
Interac
e-Transfer reconciliation files, see the
Interac
e-Transfer Settlement, Tracing, and Reports Part.
4.2	Basic Searches in OAS
Basic searches in OAS are performed using the basic search field. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: The Basic Search Field
Users can search OAS for
Interac
e-Transfer customer profiles or customer transactions by entering search characters, then selecting a category of information to search against. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Entering Search Characters and Selecting a Search Category
OAS appends the characters you enter as a parameter to each category. Searches are executed on the selection of a category. Searches can be for customers using categories specific to customer profiles, or for transactions using categories specific to transfers or transfer requests.
Customer Search Results
If a search for customer results in a match to only one customer profile, the Customer Details page for that customer is displayed. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: The Customer Details Page
If a search for customers results in matches to multiple customer profiles, a list of matching profiles is presented on a Customers page. Selecting the
Customer ID
link of any profile listed on the Customers page opens the Customer Details page for that customer. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: The Customers Page
Most basic search categories for searching by customers allow for the use of a trailing wildcard (the asterisk character 芒聙聹*芒聙聺). Searches performed using a wildcard locate information that contains the specific search characters preceding the wildcard and that contains any characters that might follow. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Using Wildcards in Searches
Transaction Search Results
If a search for transactions results in only one match to a transaction, the Transfer Details page (for transfers) or the Request Details page (for transfer requests) is displayed for that transaction. See Figure 6 and Figure 7.
Figure 6: The Transfer Details Page
Figure 7: The Request Details Page
If a search for transactions results in matches to multiple transactions, a list of matching transactions is presented on a Payments page. Selecting the
Interac Payment Reference #
link of any transaction listed on the Payments page opens the Transfer Details page for that transaction. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: The Payments Page
Note:	A basic transactions search can never result in multiple transfer request records being returned from the search and presented on a Requests page. Only a single transfer request can ever be returned through a basic transactions search and always results in the display of a Request Details page.
Categories for Basic Searches by Customers
Search OAS for customers using one of the following basic search categories:
Category
Description
Search Characteristics
Customer Registration Name
Customer芒聙聶s short name under their
Interac
e-Transfer
脗庐
sender芒聙聶s profile.
Not case-sensitive.
Accepts a trailing wildcard (芒聙聹*芒聙聺)
Customer Legal Name
Customer芒聙聶s formal name(s) [First Name, Last Name, Business Name, Trade Name] under their
Interac
e-Transfer sender芒聙聶s profile registered with you.
Not case-sensitive.
Accepts a trailing wildcard (芒聙聹*芒聙聺)
Customer Email
The email address registered in your customer芒聙聶s
Interac
e-Transfer sender芒聙聶s profile.
Tip
: If there are no search results when searching via Contact Email option, the email address provided isn芒聙聶t registered in one of your customer芒聙聶s
Interac
e-Transfer profiles. The email address may be registered at another financial institution, or the customer may have provided the wrong email address.
Not case-sensitive.
Accepts a trailing wildcard (芒聙聹*芒聙聺)
Customer Mobile
The mobile phone number registered in your customer芒聙聶s
Interac
e-Transfer sender芒聙聶s profile.
Not case-sensitive.
Accepts a trailing wildcard (芒聙聹*芒聙聺)
Customer ID
Customer芒聙聶s profile ID for their
Interac
e-Transfer sender芒聙聶s profile registered with you.
Not case-sensitive.
Accepts a trailing wildcard (芒聙聹*芒聙聺)
Contact Email
The email address associated with the
Interac
e-Transfer Send Money transfer recipient (your customer sent the
Interac
e-Transfer transaction to this email address). If the recipient of the transfer is outside of your financial institution, you only receive search results if the recipient has answered the security question for the transfer.
Not case-sensitive.
Does
not
accept a trailing wildcard (芒聙聹*芒聙聺)
Categories for Basic Searches by Transactions
Search OAS for transactions using one of the following basic search categories:
Category
Description
Search Characteristics
Interac Payment Reference
A unique reference number for Interac Send Money transactions. This is the identifier that Interac assigns to each Interac e-Transfer transaction.
Obtain this reference number from your ECCX report, under the INT REF # column, next to Interac Send Money transactions.
Case-sensitive.
Does
not
accept a trailing wildcard (芒聙聹*芒聙聺)
Interac Request Reference
A unique reference number for Interac e-Transfer Request Money transactions. This is the unique identifier that Interac assigns to each transaction.
Obtain this reference number under the INT REF # column on your ECCX Report.
Case-sensitive.
Does
not
accept a trailing wildcard (芒聙聹*芒聙聺)
FI Reference Number
The retrieval reference number for Interac Send Money transactions. This is a number generated by Central聽1 that is unique to each Interac e-Transfer transaction. It is generated each time there is an attempt to send or receive a transfer, regardless of whether the send/receive is successful. For example, if the Interac e-Transfer transaction message is rejected by Interac, or if Interac fails to receive the message due to a technical error, there is no Interac e-Transfer transaction record associated with that reference number.
Obtain this reference number from your ECCX report, under the REF NO column, next to Interac Send Money transactions.
Not case-sensitive.
Accepts a trailing wildcard (芒聙聹*芒聙聺)
4.3	Tracing
Interac
e芒聙聭Transfer Transactions in OAS
There are two ways to trace an
Interac
e-Transfer transaction in OAS, as described below.
Searching for an
Interac
e-Transfer Transaction via Reference Number
If you have the
Interac
Payment Reference, FI Reference Number, or
Interac
Request Reference associated with the
Interac
e-Transfer transaction, proceed as follows to search for the transaction:
Enter the
Interac
Payment Reference, FI Reference Number, or
Interac
Request Reference, if applicable, in the OAS search field as described in Section 4.2, Basic Searches in OAS.
View the transaction details in the Transfer Details screen. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Viewing the Transfer Details
Verify the status of the transaction. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Verifying the Status of the Interac e-Transfer Transaction
For more information on
Interac
e-Transfer transaction status types, refer to Section 4.6, Description of Interac e-Transfer Transaction Status Types.
Searching for an
Interac
e-Transfer Transaction through an
Interac
e-Transfer Profile
If you are unable to trace an
Interac
e-Transfer transaction via a reference number, search for the customer芒聙聶s
Interac
e-Transfer profile and view the customer芒聙聶s transaction history, as described below:
Enter the customer information (e.g., name, email address) in the OAS search field as described in Section 4.2, Basic Searches in OAS.
Select the appropriate drop-down menu option for searching by customer profile information and view the list customers matching your search criteria. Select the customer of interest and from the Customer Details screen, select the type of
Interac
e-Transfer transactions you want to view. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Viewing a Customer芒聙聶s Interac e-Transfer Profile
Locate the appropriate transaction from the list of transactions and verify the status. See Figure 12. For more information on
Interac
e-Transfer transaction status types, refer to Section 4.6, Description of
Interac
e-Transfer Transaction Status Types.
Figure 12: Viewing the List of Transactions
Select a reference number to view additional transfer details. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Viewing Detailed Interac e-Transfer Transaction Info
Tip
:	If you have access to the ECCX reports, but you don芒聙聶t have an
Interac
e-Transfer reference number for a transaction (for example, if you are searching for a transaction on the same day that it was created), you can use the reference number for one of the customer芒聙聶s older transactions to help you locate the customer芒聙聶s
Interac
e-Transfer profile.
4.4	Viewing Transfer Memos
You can view a memo that a Fraud Analyst has added against a transaction as part of their investigation into suspicious activity. A View link is available in the Details columns of the History section of the Transfer Details screen wherever a memo has been added against an event.
To view a transfer memo:
Open the transfer details screen as described in Section 4.3, Tracing
Interac
e-Transfer Transactions in OAS above.
In the History section of the Transfer Details screen, select
View
in the Details column. See Figure 14.
Figure 14: View Memo Option
Review the memo as it appears in the expanded memo details. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Memo Details Displayed
4.5	Viewing Remittance Details
Interac
e-Transfer for Business introduces to the
Interac
e-Transfer system the ability to send remittance details as part of a transfer芒聙聶s detail. This is useful for senders to provide, for example, the specifics of invoices they are paying though
Interac
e-Transfer. If remittance details have been provided as part of a transfer芒聙聶s details, the Transfer Details displays a link you can select to view them.
To view remittance details:
Open the transfer details screen as described in Section 4.3, Tracing
Interac
e-Transfer Transactions in OAS above.
Select the Remittance Details link on the Transfer Details screen. See Figure 16.
Figure 16: The Remittance Details Link
Review the Remittance Details that appear on the screen. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Remittance Details
4.6	Description of
Interac
e芒聙聭Transfer Transaction Status Types
During the time that a transfer is active, there are several statuses that the transfer is assigned as it is processed. The transfer status is displayed on the Transfer Details and Customer Details pages in OAS. Refer to the tables below for information on
Interac
e-Transfer transaction status types.
Interac
e-Transfer Transaction Status Types
Transfer Status
Notes
Transfer Requested
The sender requested the Interac e-Transfer. Interac has received the request.
Notification Pending
Technical difficulties are preventing the transmission of the email notification. An email notice will be sent shortly.
Contact/Recipient Notified
Interac has sent an email notice to the recipient with the relevant details about the transfer.
Contact/Recipient Notification Declined
Invalid recipient email address was provided.
Authentication Failure
Recipient validation was rejected because of too many incorrect responses to the security question.
Authentication Successful
The recipient has correctly answered the security question.
Declined
The recipient declined the transfer.
Cancelled
The sender cancelled the transfer.
Expired
The transfer expired.
Completed
The funds were deposited into the recipient芒聙聶s account.
Note:	If the state of the
Interac
e-Transfer transaction is Blocked, escalate to your corporate security team. If you are a member of your financial institution芒聙聶s corporate security team, refer to the Fraud Alert System (FAS) team or to your internal OAS Fraud Analyst (if that role is in use at your financial institution). See Chapter 6, The OAS Fraud Analyst Role.
Direct Deposit Transactions (Autodeposit, Fulfilled Requests for Money, Transfers via Account Numbering Routing)
Transfer Status
Notes
Pending direct deposit
The transfer is in the process of being deposited.
Direct deposit failed
The transfer could not be deposited. A possible cause is an account that was either invalid or closed.
Real-time initiated
A real-time payment has been initiated and received by the Interac e-Transfer system. It hasn芒聙聶t yet completed or failed. The Interac e-Transfer system commits the deposit soon after the real-time payment is initiated.
Real-time deposit completed
The Interac e-Transfer system has processed the real-time payment and the receiving participant has deposited the money in the Recipient芒聙聶s bank account.
Real-time-deposit failed
The real-time payment could not be deposited. Possible causes are an internal processing error, or the deposit/payment failed or timed-out.
4.7	Advanced Searches in OAS
Multiple search categories can be used to return a smaller, more manageable list of customers or transactions by using the Advanced Search tool in OAS.
The Advanced Searches page is opened by selecting the magnify glass icon on the Home page next to the basic search field. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: The Advanced Search Icon
Alternatively, selecting category 芒聙聹Advanced Search芒聙聺 from the basic search field芒聙聶s drop-down menu also opens the Advanced Search page. See Figure 19.
Figure 19: The Advanced Search Option
On the Advanced Search page, search by Customer (the default selection), Transfer, Request, or Autodeposit Registration. By default, a single row for defining one search criteria is displayed, containing a field with a drop-down menu of search categories and a text box for entering search text. The row has plus and minus icons for adding or deleting additional rows where additional search criteria can be defined or removed. See Figure 20.
The search categories available in the drop-down menus depend on the type of search you are performing.
Figure 20: The Advanced Search Page
Select
Search
after defining the search criteria. Search results containing multiple transaction or customer records are presented on a Customers page, Payments page, or Requests page. See Figure 21 for an example.
Figure 21: Advanced Search Results 芒聙聯 Payments
The search results can be filtered and/or ordered by the various columns and can be exported to a file in PDF or CSV file format.
Advanced Searches by Customer
If you search too broadly for customers using a basic search 芒聙聰 for example, searching by category 芒聙聹Customer Registration Name芒聙聺 for all customers with a name such as 芒聙聹John芒聙聺 (by entering the search characters 芒聙聹John*芒聙聺) 芒聙聰 your search takes a long time to execute and displays a Customers page. It may even exceed 1000 matching records, which is the maximum number of records any search in OAS returns. With search results cutting off at 1000 records, you can芒聙聶t be sure if the customer record you need to find is included in the 1000 records returned.
Also, each time you drill into the Customer Details page for a customer by selecting a
Customer ID
link from the Customers page, you can return to the Customers page by using the Back to Search Results function. See Figure 22. Selecting
Back to Search Results
re-runs the same search that returned the current Customers page displayed. This can be a frustrating user experience if that search is a long-running one.
Figure 22: The Back to Search Results Function
To optimize customer searches, use the Advanced Search page. See Figure 23.
Select
Customer
.
Add multiple search criteria.
Select
Search
.
Figure 23: Performing Customer Searches on the Advanced Search Page

Advanced Search by Transfer
If you search and select a customer from the Customers list, but find it takes an excessively long time for the Customer Details page to display, it芒聙聶s because the customer has a large number of transfers or transfer requests, and OAS is taking a long time to gather and display them. Also, if you drill into the Transfer Details of a specific transaction, each time you return to the Customer Details page, OAS re-runs the long running search of the customer芒聙聶s transactions. If the number of the customer芒聙聶s transfers exceeds 1000 for any specific category of transaction, only 1000 records are returned, and the transfer you need to see may not be in those 1000 records.
To narrow down the time it takes to search and display a customer芒聙聶s transfers, copy the customer芒聙聶s profile ID while you are on the Customer Details page, then use the Advanced Search page to optimize transfer searches. See Figure 24.
Select
Transfer
.
Enter the customer芒聙聶s profile ID as the search value under either the
Payee Customer ID
category (for searching incoming transfers) or the
Payer Customer ID
category (for searching for outgoing transfers).
Add category
Payment Date
and select a date 芒聙聰 or a date range 芒聙聰 to restrict the transfers returned from the search to a manageable number.
Select
Search
.
Figure 24: Performing Transfer Searches on the Advanced Search Page

Category

Bulk Request Reference Number
N/A
N/A

Recipient芒聙聶s email address used in any e-Transfer sent by a customer.

Uses a date selection tool.

Not case-sensitive.

The ID assigned by Interac to an e-Transfer.
Case-sensitive.

NA

Not case-sensitive.
Accepts a trailing wildcard (芒聙聹*芒聙聺)
Payer Customer ID
Customer profile ID for their sender of an e-Transfer.
Not case-sensitive.
Accepts a trailing wildcard (芒聙聹*芒聙聺)

Case-sensitive.
Does

Advanced Search by Requests for Transfer
Similar to searching by transfer, you can use the Advanced Search page to search for transfer requests:
Select
Request
.
Add multiple search criteria.
Select
Search
.
Results from the requests search can include incoming transfer requests that aren't otherwise found in OAS on the Customer Details page (there you only see Sent Money Request(s) sections, not Received Money Request(s) sections).
If multiple requests are returned from the search, the Requests page appears, from which Request Details can be opened for any listed request by selecting the Request Reference # link. See Figure 25.
Figure 25:
The Requests Page

4.8	Archived Data Searches
Regular searches in OAS allows you to search for
Interac
e-Transfer transactions up to two years old. OAS also provides a feature called Archived Data Search, giving your financial institutions the ability to search transactions that are over two years old. See Figure 26.
Using the Archived Data Search page, you can search for older transactions by choosing a basic search category:
Sender Customer ID
Recipient Customer ID
Sender and Recipient Customer ID
Figure 26: The Archived Data Search page
Additional fields related to the selected search category allow for a specific search. Partial matches result if wildcards are used. See Figure 27.
Figure 27: The Archived Data Search page
Selecting
Search
executes the search with the search results displayed on an Archived e-Transfer page. See Figure 28. Search results can be filtered and/or ordered by the various columns and are exportable to a file in PDF or CSV format.
Figure 28: The Archived eTransfers page
END
